cards.question: The first magnet was discovered by a Greek shepherd named Magnes.
cards.text_html: The first magnet was discovered by a Greek shepherd named Magnes.

cards.question: Magnet: An object that attracts substances like iron, cobalt, and nickel is called a magnet.
cards.text_html: Magnet: An object that attracts substances like iron, cobalt, and nickel is called a magnet.

cards.question: Magnets are made of materials that attract objects made of certain substances like iron, cobalt, and nickel.
cards.text_html: Magnets are made of materials that attract objects made of certain substances like iron, cobalt, and nickel.

cards.question: Magnets can be naturally occurring or man-made. The man-made magnets are called artificial magnets.
cards.text_html: Magnets can be naturally occurring or man-made. The man-made magnets are called artificial magnets.

cards.question: Temporary magnets: Magnets that retain their magnetic properties only for a short period of time are called temporary magnets.
cards.text_html: Temporary magnets: Magnets that retain their magnetic properties only for a short period of time are called temporary magnets.

cards.question: Permanent magnets: Magnets that retain their magnetic properties for a long period of time are called permanent magnets.
cards.text_html: Permanent magnets: Magnets that retain their magnetic properties for a long period of time are called permanent magnets.

cards.question: Magnets can be found as horseshoes, rings, cylindrical, or bar shapes. ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
cards.text_html:
Magnets can be found as horseshoes, rings, cylindrical, or bar shapes.

 ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

cards.question: The materials which are attracted by a magnet are called magnetic materials . For example iron, steel, nickel, and cobalt.
cards.text_html: The materials which are attracted by a magnet are called magnetic materials. For example iron, steel, nickel, and cobalt.

cards.question: Magnets are usually made from magnetic materials such as iron and steel.
cards.text_html: Magnets are usually made from magnetic materials such as iron and steel.

cards.question: The materials which are not attracted by a magnet are called non-magnetic materials. For example – wood, plastic, rubber, etc.
cards.text_html: The materials which are not attracted by a magnet are called non-magnetic materials. For example – wood, plastic, rubber, etc.

cards.question: A magnet has always two poles. The two poles of the magnet are near its free ends. One pole of the magnet is called the north pole and the other pole of the magnet is called the south pole.
cards.text_html: A magnet has always two poles. The two poles of the magnet are near its free ends. One pole of the magnet is called the north pole and the other pole of the magnet is called the south pole.

cards.question: Magnetic forces are the strongest at the poles.
cards.text_html: Magnetic forces are the strongest at the poles.

cards.question: The same type of magnetic pole is called like poles whereas the different types of magnetic poles are called, unlike poles.
cards.text_html: The same type of magnetic pole is called like poles whereas the different types of magnetic poles are called, unlike poles.

cards.question: Properties of magnets: A freely suspended magnet always points in the north-south direction. Like magnetic poles repel each other while unlike magnetic poles attract each other.
cards.text_html:
Properties of magnets:
  1. A freely suspended magnet always points in the north-south direction.
  2. Like magnetic poles repel each other while unlike magnetic poles attract each other.

cards.question: If we break a bar magnet in the middle, we would get two pieces, each having a the North Pole and the South Pole. We could go on breaking the magnet into smaller pieces, and every time we would get both the poles in each piece.
cards.text_html: If we break a bar magnet in the middle, we would get two pieces, each having a the North Pole and the South Pole. We could go on breaking the magnet into smaller pieces, and every time we would get both the poles in each piece.

cards.question: If a magnet is allowed to move freely, it comes to rest in a direction very close to the Earth’s North-South direction. This property of a magnet was used to find directions on the surface of the Earth by travelers.
cards.text_html: If a magnet is allowed to move freely, it comes to rest in a direction very close to the Earth’s North-South direction. This property of a magnet was used to find directions on the surface of the Earth by travelers.

cards.question: An instrument with a magnet that is used to find directions is called a magnetic compass.
cards.text_html: An instrument with a magnet that is used to find directions is called a magnetic compass.

cards.question: The simplest and the easiest way of making a magnet is by the ‘ stroking method ’. If we stroke an iron bar with one end of the bar magnet repeatedly in the same direction, the iron bar becomes a magnet.
cards.text_html: The simplest and the easiest way of making a magnet is by the ‘stroking method’. If we stroke an iron bar with one end of the bar magnet repeatedly in the same direction, the iron bar becomes a magnet.

cards.question: Magnets can also be made by using electricity. Such magnets are called electromagnets.
cards.text_html: Magnets can also be made by using electricity. Such magnets are called electromagnets.

cards.question: When like poles of the magnets (N-N or S-S) are brought close to each other, they repel. This is called repulsion.
cards.text_html: When like poles of the magnets (N-N or S-S) are brought close to each other, they repel. This is called repulsion.

cards.question: When unlike poles of the magnets (N - S or S - N) are brought close to each other, they attract. This is called attraction.
cards.text_html: When unlike poles of the magnets (N - S or S - N) are brought close to each other, they attract. This is called attraction.

cards.question: A magnet can lose its properties due to the following activities.
cards.text_html: A magnet can lose its properties due to the following activities.

cards.question: How to store magnets properly: The magnets should be stored in pairs with their unlike poles on the same side and bars of soft iron kept across their ends. The bar magnets in pairs should be stored by separating them with a piece of wood and keeping soft iron bars across their opposite ends to prevent loss of magnetism. The horseshoe magnets are stored properly by just keeping a bar of soft iron across their ends.
cards.text_html:
How to store magnets properly:
  1. The magnets should be stored in pairs with their unlike poles on the same side and bars of soft iron kept across their ends.
  2. The bar magnets in pairs should be stored by separating them with a piece of wood and keeping soft iron bars across their opposite ends to prevent loss of magnetism.
  3. The horseshoe magnets are stored properly by just keeping a bar of soft iron across their ends.

cards.question: Uses of magnets: Magnets are used to hold objects like stickers, pencil box lids, pins, etc. They are used to separate discarded iron and steel objects from other wastes. They are used for making compasses. They are used in telephones, doorbells, mixers and grinders, electric motors, generators, television sets, etc.
cards.text_html:
Uses of magnets:
  1. Magnets are used to hold objects like stickers, pencil box lids, pins, etc.
  2. They are used to separate discarded iron and steel objects from other wastes.
  3. They are used for making compasses.
  4. They are used in telephones, doorbells, mixers and grinders, electric motors, generators, television sets, etc.
